sa:actions.rdfs:label: Attack
sa:actions.sa:description:
Make a weapon attack. If proficient with the weapon, roll 1d20 + Proficiency Bonus + Ability Modifier + Miscellaneous Bonuses.

Ability Modifier Selection: Ranged weapons use Dexterity modifier; melee weapons use Strength modifier. Melee weapons with the finesse tag let you use either Dexterity or Strength modifier (your choice).

Damage Calculation: Weapon Dice + Ability Modifier. Examples: longsword (1d8 + 2 with 14 STR); .45 Falcon Pistol (1d8 + 3 with 16 DEX).

Ammunition Consumption: Weapons with dice-based ammunition expend dice equal to weapon base damage (or amount used in Burst). Weapons with number-based ammunition expend one attack per attack roll. If a weapon has fewer dice remaining in its magazine than its base damage, damage is reduced to match available dice.

Mercy Rule: When reducing a target to 0 wounds or below, you may choose to incapacitate them instead, leaving them unconscious with 1 wound remaining. Exception: You cannot use mercy with brutal or horrifying weapons.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Hide
sa:actions.sa:description: As part of a move, roll a Stealth check. You must be unobserved or at least lightly obscured to hide. A creature remains unaware of your presence unless its passive or active Perception equals or exceeds your Stealth check.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Ambush
sa:actions.sa:description:
As part of a single attack roll with one weapon, roll a Stealth check at disadvantage. If successful, you remain in Stealth after the attack.

Weapon Tag Interactions: Covert tag negates the disadvantage on your Stealth check. Loud tag: This action cannot be used with weapons bearing this tag.

Limitations: You can only make one attack with this action, even if you normally make multiple attacks.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Defensive Move
sa:actions.sa:description:
Declare this action and spend your action before moving. During that move, designate one attack against you to gain +5 bonus to AC against that attack.

Overwatch Interaction: If you enter an area covered by an Overwatch action during your move, gain advantage on your Dexterity save.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Burst
sa:actions.sa:requirements: A ranged attack gained from an Attack action
sa:actions.sa:description:
When making your first Burst attack of the round, declare whether you will move or forfeit your move. If you combine Burst attacks with movement, your attacks are made at disadvantage.

Damage Limits: Burst attacks can exceed the weapon's base damage but cannot exceed its maximum burst damage. They consume additional dice from the weapon's clip or magazine.

Precision Damage: Never added to Burst attacks (e.g., Sneak Attack and similar effects are excluded).

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Overwatch
sa:actions.sa:description:
Designate a cone from your square, up to 45 degrees wide. Line-of-sight and line-of-effect obstacles block the cone. The cone reaches up to half your weapon’s range (minimum 30 yards).
Enemies up to your weapon’s RoF that take an attack action, cast a spell, or move into the cone with line of effect to you must make a Dexterity save vs your Ranged Ability DC. Targets that fail take full damage; those who succeed take half.
Spend your weapon’s RoF in ammo. If no valid targets appear, no ammo is spent.
You cannot move after declaring Overwatch. Forced movement ends it.
If using a melee weapon, you may affect up to Dex mod + Proficiency Bonus enemies that enter your threatened area and fail a Dex save vs your Melee Ability DC. Melee Overwatch does not end on forced movement.
Overwatch or suppressive fire with brutal or terrifying weapons makes the area difficult terrain.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Arc Attack
sa:actions.sa:requirements: An autocannon, gatling weapon, or flamethrower-type weapon
sa:actions.sa:description:
Designate a cone from your square, up to 45 degrees wide, extending to half your weapon's range (minimum 30 yards). The cone is blocked by line-of-sight and line-of-effect obstacles.

Requirements: You must spend double your weapon's maximum burst dice in ammo. If the magazine contains insufficient ammunition, you cannot make an Arc Attack.

Attack and Damage: Make an attack roll with all bonuses. All entities in the cone take normal damage as if hit by the attack.

Restrictions: You cannot move after declaring Arc Attack. Precision damage is never added to Arc Attacks.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Cleave
sa:actions.sa:requirements: An attack from an Attack action
sa:actions.sa:description:
If multiple adjacent enemies are within reach of your melee weapon, roll one attack and damage roll. Choose a primary target; damage is reduced by 2 for each additional target hit by this attack.

Cleaver Weapons: Weapons with the Cleaver tag do not reduce damage for additional targets.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Focus Energies
sa:actions.sa:description:
Spend your action to gain the Focused condition. You calm your mind to focus on a spell, magical effect, or similar ability.

Effect: The Focused condition affects various spells and features throughout the game.

Maintaining Focus: Taking damage while Focused requires a Constitution save (DC = 8 + damage taken) to maintain the condition. Failure removes the Focused condition.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Execute
sa:actions.sa:description:
If you are within melee reach of a paralyzed, unconscious, helpless (GM discretion), or heavily wounded target, you can kill it with an automatic critical strike.

Horrifying Weapon Response: If you use a horrifying weapon, all witnesses must make a Wisdom save vs. fear (DC = 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Charisma modifier) or become frightened. Frightened creatures have disadvantage on attacks against you until you are successfully hit by an attack.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Help
sa:actions.sa:description:
Assist with Ability Check: Assist another creature with a task. The target gains advantage on their next relevant ability check made before your next turn.

Assist with Attack (Alternative): Aid an ally attacking a creature within 5 feet of you. If you can make multiple attacks, grant advantage on the same number of ally attacks (using feints or distractions to help your ally hit).

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Multi-Attack
sa:actions.sa:description:
If wielding more than one weapon, spend a bonus action to make attacks with an additional weapon. Only one weapon adds your ability modifier — choose which weapon before rolling.

Weapon Restrictions: Secondary weapons must be light or mounted.

Combinations: Can be combined with Burst, Cleave, and Knockdown.

Example: A gunslinger with two pistols uses their action for one attack and bonus action for attacks with the second pistol.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Reload
sa:actions.sa:description:
Martial Weapons: Reload as part of your action (standard reload).

System Weapons: Reload requires a full-round action.

Special Rules: Some system and crew weapons have unique reload mechanics—consult the weapon's description.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Information System Attack
sa:actions.sa:description:
Make an Information System Attack using the following formula: 1d20 + Proficiency Bonus + Intelligence Modifier + Equipment Bonus.

Damage: Based on the program or hack used (consult program description).

Requirements: A hacking kit is required.

Proficiency Benefit: If you gain double proficiency with hacking kits, add double your Proficiency Bonus to the attack roll.

Multiple Attacks: Once you begin making Information System Attacks, you may continue making additional attacks until your bandwidth is spent (subject to your available bandwidth pool).

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Command Drones or Computers Using Bandwidth
sa:actions.sa:description:
Spend up to your available bandwidth to issue orders to drones or computers under your command.

Action Limitations: Regardless of bandwidth spent, each drone can take only 1 action and move once per round.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Grapple
sa:actions.sa:description:
Target a creature within reach and make an opposed check: your Athletics vs. their Athletics or Acrobatics (their choice). If you win, the target becomes Grappled.

Grappler Status: You are not grappled (only the target is).

Grappled Condition Effects:
• Speed becomes 0; speed bonuses do not apply
• Ends if you (the grappler) become incapacitated
• Ends if any effect moves the grappled creature away (e.g., Thunderwave)

Movement: You can move, dragging the grappled creature with you, but your speed is halved unless the creature is two or more sizes smaller than you.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Shove
sa:actions.sa:description:
Make a special melee attack against a creature within reach and no more than one size category larger. Choose one of two outcomes: knock the target prone or push it away.

Attack Roll: Make a Strength (Athletics) check opposed by the target's Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) [target chooses].

Unarmed vs. Armed Penalty: If you are unarmed and the target is armed, you roll your Athletics check at disadvantage. If your check fails, the target may make a free melee attack against you.

sa:actions.rdfs:label: Cast a Spell
sa:actions.sa:description:
Spell Point/Slot Costs:
• Devotee/Arcanist: Spend spell points equal to spell level (e.g., Magic Missile at level 3 costs 3 points)
• Occultist: Spend a spell slot

Casting Interruption: If you take ongoing damage or are damaged via reaction/ready action while casting, make a casting check (Casting Ability + Proficiency Bonus vs. DC 10 + spell point cost). If you fail, you lose the spell points.

Exceptions:
• Reaction spells are not subject to interruption checks
• If you have the Focused condition, you may abandon it as a reaction to gain advantage on a casting or concentration check
